metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the exact file-and-line inventory of all workflow files that need each action bumped (the actual count exceeds the stated '12 files') and confirm PR #119's diff covers every instance, including reconcile-bridge.yml, reconcile-bridge-canary.yml, and terraform-drift.yml which are still at @v4 for checkout/cache/credentials.
findings.finding: The plan states '12 changed workflow files' but the repository contains at least 13 distinct files with `actions/checkout` references alone (across mirror-guard, release, external-integration, _optionality, reconcile-bridge, test, codeql, verify-identity, eval-plan-review-container, eval, gerrit-verify, terraform-drift, reconcile-bridge-canary), and multiple other files need cache/setup-python/upload-artifact bumps. If the implementer lands only Dependabot PR #119's diff — which was generated from whatever version baseline Dependabot observed — files at intermediate versions (e.g., reconcile-bridge.yml at checkout@v4, reconcile-bridge-canary.yml at setup-python@v5) may be absent from that PR's diff and left behind, causing AC #1 to fail silently.
findings.location: Scope section ('12 affected workflow files') and AC #1
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit per-file inventory table to the plan (or a grep-based AC verification step: `grep -r 'actions/checkout@' .github/workflows/ | grep -v '@v7'` should return empty after the change), and confirm PR #119's diff includes all 15 workflow files, not just the 12 stated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and document that the `aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials` bump is v4→v6 (two-major skip), and confirm that neither v5 nor v6 changed any inputs/defaults used in `terraform-drift.yml` (role-to-assume, aws-region).
findings.finding: The plan states 'aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials → v6', but the current repo has v4 (terraform-drift.yml:36), making this a two-major skip (v4→v6) rather than a single-major bump. The plan's 'Scope' section asserts '7 are clean runtime bumps with no input/default changes touching our usage' — this count and its cleanness characterization apply implicitly to configure-aws-credentials, but a two-major jump (skipping v5) carries higher risk of undocumented input/behavior changes than a single-major bump, and is not called out.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that `actions/setup-python` is already at v6 in most files; explicitly name `reconcile-bridge.yml` and `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml` as the two files where setup-python v5→v6 is still required.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'actions/setup-python → v6' as a new major bump being introduced by this PR, but grep evidence shows setup-python@v6 is already the version used in the majority of workflow files (20+ occurrences across mirror-guard, release, external-integration, gerrit-verify, _optionality, test, verify-identity, eval-plan-review-container, eval). Only reconcile-bridge.yml (line 124) and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml (line 85) still use v5. The plan's framing implies a uniform pre-bump baseline that does not match codebase reality, which could cause a reviewer to miss that the two reconcile-bridge workflows need the setup-python bump while assuming all files are in scope.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, revise the setup-python entry to: '`actions/setup-python` → v6 (already at v6 in 10 files; v5 still in reconcile-bridge.yml and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml — these two need the bump)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly verify that `eval.yml`'s `actions/upload-artifact@v4` → @v7 multi-major skip (not just v6→v7) has no breaking input/default changes for the artifact name and path used in that workflow, and document this in the plan's risk assessment.
findings.finding: The plan asserts all 7 non-checkout action bumps are 'clean runtime bumps with no input/default changes touching our usage,' but eval.yml uses `actions/upload-artifact@v4` (not the @v6 that release.yml uses). The target is @v7, making this a 3-major skip (v4→v7) for eval.yml. Between v4 and v7, upload-artifact changed its default compression level, artifact retention defaults, and introduced a new storage backend in v4 itself. The plan does not acknowledge this specific multi-major path for eval.yml.
findings.location: Scope section ('7 are clean runtime bumps with no input/default changes') and AC #1 (upload-artifact@v7)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note in the Scope section confirming that reconcile-bridge.yml and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml are at @v4 (two-major jump to v7), and explicitly vet that actions/checkout v5 introduced no breaking changes to the `persist-credentials: true` + `fetch-depth: 0` + worktree-push pattern used by those files.
findings.finding: actions/checkout in reconcile-bridge.yml (line 75) and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml (line 68) is currently at @v4, not @v5 like the rest of the codebase. These two files jump two major versions (v4→v7). The plan discusses only the v6 persist-credentials worktree fix but does not acknowledge these files start from v4, nor does it confirm v5 introduced no breaking changes to the `persist-credentials`+`fetch-depth: 0` combination used by both files.
findings.location: Scope section — 'The one risk — actions/checkout v6 moved persist-credentials'; reconcile-bridge.yml line 75; reconcile-bridge-canary.yml line 68

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the Scope section, explicitly identify which 7 actions are 'clean runtime bumps', confirm upload-artifact's v5 and v6 changelogs introduce no breaking changes to the `name`/`path`/`if-no-files-found` inputs used in eval.yml, and note that eval.yml starts at v4 (three-major jump).
findings.finding: eval.yml line 134 uses actions/upload-artifact@v4, while release.yml line 43 already uses @v6. The plan targets upload-artifact→v7 but describes only '7 clean runtime bumps with no input/default changes touching our usage' without specifying which 7. eval.yml is jumping three major versions (v4→v7), skipping v5 and v6 — any breaking input/output changes in those intermediate majors are unaddressed by the plan.
findings.location: Scope section — 'The one risk … 7 are clean runtime bumps'; eval.yml line 134

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly confirm the multi-major jumps (upload-artifact@v4→v7 in eval.yml; checkout@v4→v7 in reconcile-bridge.yml and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml) by checking v5 and v6 changelogs/release notes for any input, default, or behavior changes that touch the specific `with:` parameters used in those workflow steps.
findings.finding: The plan's blanket 'clean runtime bumps with no input/default changes touching our usage' characterization does not explicitly account for multi-major jumps: `eval.yml` goes from `upload-artifact@v4` to `v7` (three majors), and both `reconcile-bridge.yml` and `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml` go from `checkout@v4` to `v7` (three majors, spanning the v6.0.0 worktree-credential regression the plan specifically flags). While the target versions are mechanistically correct, the plan's vetting claim is imprecise — a three-major skip may involve intermediate input renames, default-behavior changes, or deprecation removals that a 'next-major-only' review would miss.
findings.location: Scope section — '7 are clean runtime bumps with no input/default changes touching our usage'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add a brief note such as: 'Three workflow files skip multiple majors (reconcile-bridge.yml and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml: checkout v4→v7; eval.yml: upload-artifact v4→v7); intermediate changelogs for v5 and v6 of each were reviewed and confirm no input/default changes affect the parameters used in those steps.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite the specific checkout v7 release note or commit that confirms the `worktrees/*` includeIf fix for persist-credentials (e.g., actions/checkout CHANGELOG or GitHub issue link), to ground the safety claim for reconcile-bridge's worktree-push path.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the checkout v7 `persist-credentials` + worktree push fix works 'via a `worktrees/*` includeIf', but cites no release note, commit, or issue reference to support this technical claim. reconcile-bridge.yml and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml both use `persist-credentials: true` + `git worktree add` to push to origin/tickets — if the claim is wrong, the commit-back step silently fails to authenticate.
findings.location: Scope section: 'fixed in v6.0.1+/v7 via a `worktrees/*` includeIf'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a reference such as: 'Fixed in actions/checkout v6.0.1 (actions/checkout#1872 / release notes vX.Y.Z): credential helper is now registered with `includeIf gitdir:../worktrees/` so mounted worktrees inherit GITHUB_TOKEN creds.' This lets reviewers verify the claim independently.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For each of the 8 action bumps, add a brief citation (changelog URL or 'no breaking inputs' note) confirming no input/default changes affect the workflows' actual usage — especially for the multi-major skips (upload-artifact v4/v6→v7, download-artifact v7→v8).
findings.finding: The plan asserts '7 are clean runtime bumps with no input/default changes touching our usage' but does not cite any source (changelogs, release notes, or migration guides) for the two multi-major skips: upload-artifact (repo has both v4 in eval.yml and v6 in release.yml, targeting v7) and download-artifact (v7 → v8). upload-artifact v4→v7 spans three majors and download-artifact v7→v8 spans one; each major boundary can introduce input renames, default changes, or artifact retention/scope API changes.
findings.location: Scope section: '7 are clean runtime bumps with no input/default changes touching our usage'; AC line 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either pin the 8 bumped actions to their full-length commit SHAs (consistent with the existing `gerrit-verify.yaml` SHA-pinning pattern), or explicitly document why floating major-version tags are acceptable for these specific actions while SHA-pins are used elsewhere.
findings.finding: The plan introduces floating major-version tags (`@v7`, `@v6`, `@v8`, etc.) for all 8 actions, but the repo already uses SHA-pinned action references as an established security pattern (e.g., gerrit-verify.yaml pins `lfreleng-actions/checkout-gerrit-change-action@f87b90a3370e62dad310797fedc7fd3700c75832` and `actions/setup-python@ece7cb06caefa5fff74198d8649806c4678c61a1`). The plan does not acknowledge or justify the inconsistency with this existing approach.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the PR pins the floating @v7 tag'; AC line 1 (all targets listed as floating tags)
findings.suggested_fix: Use the SHA corresponding to each target tag (e.g., `actions/checkout@<sha>  # v7.x.y`) so the version is both human-readable and tamper-evident, matching the pattern already present in gerrit-verify.yaml.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each of AC1–3 (e.g., a grep anchored to each action name for AC1, `yamllint` or `python -c 'import yaml,sys,glob; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in glob.glob(".github/workflows/*.yml")]'` for AC2, and `git diff --name-only HEAD~1 | grep -v '^.github/workflows/'` asserting empty output for AC3).
findings.finding: ACs 1–3 name the outcomes to verify (version strings updated, YAML valid, no out-of-scope files) but provide no concrete proving commands. Without them, an executor must invent the verification approach, risking inconsistent or incomplete checks.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' section:
```
## Verification
# AC1 – all 8 version strings present
grep -rE 'actions/checkout@v7|actions/setup-python@v6|github/codeql-action/(init|analyze)@v4|actions/upload-artifact@v7|actions/cache@v6|actions/download-artifact@v8|aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v6|hashicorp/setup-terraform@v4' .github/workflows/ | wc -l  # expect ≥8 distinct matches

# AC2 – valid YAML
python3 -c "import yaml,glob; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in glob.glob('.github/workflows/*.yml')]"

# AC3 – no out-of-scope files
git diff --name-only HEAD | grep -v '^.github/workflows/'  # expect empty
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cardinality check to AC1's proving command (e.g., `git diff --name-only HEAD | grep '^.github/workflows/' | wc -l` and assert the result equals 12) or drop the specific count if it is approximate.
findings.finding: AC1 asserts a cardinality of exactly '12 affected workflow files' but provides no command that checks this count; a grep on action-name patterns alone cannot confirm the number of distinct files touched, so the '12 files' claim is unverified.
findings.suggested_fix: Either enumerate the 12 files explicitly in the plan (makes the count verifiable) or replace '12' with 'all' and rely on the per-action grep to catch any missed file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC1 (or add a sibling criterion) to verify that no prior major-version references (e.g. `@v5`, `@v3`) remain in `.github/workflows/` for the 8 bumped actions, not just that new-version references exist.
findings.finding: AC1 verifies only that target major versions are present ('all 8 actions reference their target major versions') but does not verify removal of prior version references. For a migration, both the old string's absence and the new string's presence should be checked; a stray old-version reference in an untouched workflow step would pass AC1 while leaving the migration incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: '…and no references to previous major versions of these 8 actions remain in any .github/workflows/*.yml file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan (e.g., 'revert the Gerrit change and re-pin to current versions' or 'submit a follow-up patch restoring prior tags') so the rollback path is documented and not left implicit.
findings.finding: The plan identifies the checkout v6 persist-credentials risk and asserts it is resolved by pinning @v7, but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan if that assumption is wrong or if any of the other 7 action bumps cause unexpected CI failures after the change lands on main.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'If CI fails after landing, submit a Gerrit revert patch restoring the 8 action references to their pre-bump versions (checkout@v5, setup-python@v5, etc.) as listed in the original PR diff.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update AC1 to state '13 changed workflow YAML files' and enumerate all 13 explicitly (add reconcile-bridge-canary.yml to the named list or provide the full list in the Scope section).
findings.finding: AC1 states '12 changed workflow YAML files' but grep enumerates 13 distinct files under .github/workflows/ that contain at least one of the 8 target actions: _optionality.yml, codeql.yml, eval-plan-review-container.yml, eval.yml, external-integration.yml, gerrit-verify.yaml, mirror-guard.yml, reconcile-bridge-canary.yml, reconcile-bridge.yml, release.yml, terraform-drift.yml, test.yml, verify-identity.yaml.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1 — '12 changed workflow YAML files'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '12 affected workflow files' with '13 affected workflow files' and list them: _optionality.yml, codeql.yml, eval-plan-review-container.yml, eval.yml, external-integration.yml, gerrit-verify.yaml, mirror-guard.yml, reconcile-bridge-canary.yml, reconcile-bridge.yml, release.yml, terraform-drift.yml, test.yml, verify-identity.yaml.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
